1.description: Repo trades on a securities dealer balance sheet. This figure illustrates how repo activity in various segments is recorded on a balance sheet. Trades booked on the asset side are reverse repos and those on the liability side are repos. Tri-party repos (TPR) are dealer-to-client (DtC) trades that are cleared and settled on the TPR settlement platform and as such are cash-driven trades only. NonTPR are all DtC trades that are not cleared and settled on the TPR settlement platform, and include both cash- and securities-driven trades.

2.description: Repo and reverse repo, by asset class. This figure shows the total amount of outstanding repo and reverse repo activity over time. Repo is the “total securities out” value of financing activity reported in FR 2004C, and reverse repo is the “total securities in” value. Agency MBS is agency mortgage-backed securities. Source: FR 2004C.

3.description: Interdealer shares of MIX Treasury repo. This figure plots the estimated share of total MIX outstanding repo activity involving Treasuries that is cleared by FICC. MIX is the mixed segment where both cash- and securities-driven trades are settled. Source: FICC, FR2004C, and authors' calculations.

4.description: Repo activity involving on-the-run Treasuries by primary dealers, 2008. Panel A illustrates the daily total outstanding repo involving on-the-run Treasury securities by primary dealers over 2008. Panel B shows the 25th, 50th, and 75th percentiles of the distribution of these amounts across primary dealers for each month in 2008. Source: FR2004SI and authors' calculations.
